ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'chicken salad' class (FOODON_00002473); this record is a specific variant with egg and four customisable toppings served without sauce. Closest parent match accepted as a more specific code is not available.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP maps to chicken salad as a composite dish. The specific variant with egg and four toppings without sauce is not individually coded; closest parent match used.

main_ingredients: assorted salad toppings (e.g. cucumber, tomato, carrot, corn, bell pepper)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g, representing a full plate of mixed greens topped with cooked chicken, one egg, and four vegetable toppings served without sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Salad with Egg (4 Toppings), No Sauce — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Chicken salad with egg (4 toppings), no sauce' (cr_id F08010017). All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (grilled chicken breast + hard-boiled egg + mixed greens) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific composite salad. GI estimated at approximately 32 based on analogous low-GI mixed salad with lean protein and egg from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dominant carbohydrate contributors are mixed salad vegetables and egg, both of which have very low GI values (GI < 20 for non-starchy vegetables; egg GI ~0). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (32) × carbohydrate per serving (6.8 g from 300 g portion) / 100 = 2.2 g, confirming very low GL. Estimation is conservative and defensible.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium at 164 mg per 100 g without sauce
hypertension.guidance: This salad without sauce provides only 164 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 492 mg per 300 g serving), well within DASH dietary targets; patients with hypertension should continue to avoid adding high-sodium dressings or sauces and select low-sodium toppings such as cucumber, tomato, and carrot rather than pickled or processed items.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.27 g/100 g), low GI (~32), high protein (7.46 g/100 g), and dietary fibre (2.02 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 2.27 g carbohydrate per 100 g, an estimated GI of 32, and 7.46 g protein per 100 g, this salad produces a minimal glycaemic response and supports postprandial glucose control; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid high-carbohydrate toppings such as corn, croutons, or sweetened dried fruit to preserve the favourable glycaemic profile.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.70 g/100 g), low energy density (63 kcal/100 g), and high dietary fibre (2.02 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content, high fibre, and lean protein from chicken and egg make this salad a heart-healthy option aligned with AHA dietary guidelines; patients with cardiovascular disease should ensure the chicken is grilled or poached rather than fried and should avoid high-fat cheese or processed meat toppings.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low saturated fat, dietary fibre, and anti-inflammatory micronutrients from vegetable toppings
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The low saturated fat, dietary fibre, and potential vitamin C and vitamin D contributions from vegetable toppings and egg yolk support anti-inflammatory goals in rheumatoid arthritis; patients should monitor nightshade-family toppings (tomato, bell pepper) if individual joint sensitivity is suspected.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Salad with Egg (4 Toppings), No Sauce is a Western-style composed salad dish consisting of cooked chicken — typically grilled or poached — served over a bed of fresh mixed salad greens, accompanied by one hard-boiled or sliced egg and a choice of four additional toppings such as cucumber, tomato, carrot, corn, bell pepper, or onion. The dish is served without any dressing or sauce, making it one of the lowest-calorie and lowest-sodium salad options available. Per 100 g, it provides 63 kcal, 7.46 g protein, 2.68 g fat, 2.27 g carbohydrate, 2.02 g dietary fibre, and 164 mg sodium. The texture is crisp from the fresh greens and toppings, tender from the chicken, and creamy from the egg. The flavour profile is mild and clean without sauce. Clinically, this dish is highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its very low carbohydrate load and estimated low glycaemic index of approximately 32. It is also appropriate for hypertension management given its low sodium content without sauce, and for cardiovascular health given its low saturated fat and high fibre. The dish is halal-certified, gluten-free, and dairy-free in its standard form. It is commonly consumed as a light lunch or dinner and is popular in Singapore's health-conscious café and salad bar settings. The HPB Singapore Foodo database lists this item under customisable mixed dishes.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this exact dish (cr_id F08010017) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, which is the approved GI source.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.70 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy for grilled chicken breast, hard-boiled egg, and mixed salad greens, as the HPB Foodo database does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. This value is consistent with the health_suitability note in the input record. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data for this composite customisable salad dish are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this exact formulation as of 2026. This is due to limited published data for such a customisable preparation. The source consulted (HPB Singapore Foodo) confirms the unavailability of these specific detailed nutrient values.
